summary: Matched several functions in src/melee/vi/vi1202, most notably the full vi1202_OnEnter cutscene setup path, and renamed the scene callbacks at 80321294/80321300/803218E0 to vi1202_RunFrame, vi1202_OnEnter, and vi1202_OnFrame. The PR also adjusted vi1202 data typing and prototypes, added local JObj setter clones for matching, rewrote several crowd/audio helper paths to match, and fixed copied HSD_JObjSetScaleY_2/Z_2 helpers in vi1201v1 to assign the correct scale axes. decomp.dev reported 6 new vi1202 matches and +3068 matched bytes overall.

key_files.reason: Main matching work: implemented/renamed vi1202_RunFrame, vi1202_OnEnter, vi1202_OnFrame; added archive/light/camera/model setup; changed local data types; introduced helper functions and control-flow rewrites for matching.
Updated exported prototypes and callback names, and changed several parameters from int to u32 to match usage.
Renamed symbols at 80321294, 80321300, and 803218E0 from un_* names to vi1202_* names.
Updated MN_CUTSCENE_GIGADEFEATED MinorSceneHandler to use vi1202_OnFrame and vi1202_OnEnter.
Corrected local HSD_JObjSetScaleY_2 and HSD_JObjSetScaleZ_2 helper assignments from scale.x to scale.y/scale.z.

what_changed: Renamed un_80321294 to vi1202_RunFrame, un_80321300_OnEnter to vi1202_OnEnter, and un_803218E0_OnFrame to vi1202_OnFrame in symbols, header declarations, and the Giga Bowser defeated cutscene scene table.

Implemented the large vi1202_OnEnter setup function, including audio/effects initialization, archive symbol loading for Vi1202.dat, TyKoopaR.dat, and GmRgStnd.dat, light and camera object creation, camera animation hookup, scene model spawning, Koopa model loading, and model transform adjustment.

Changed vi1202_UnkStruct field xC from s32 to u32 and propagated unsigned argument types through un_80321D30, un_8032201C, and un_8032233C where IDs are compared against xC or passed to ftLib lookups.

Changed the vi1202 archive globals from externally declared DynamicModelDesc***/HSD_JObj* style declarations to static SceneDesc*/HSD_Joint*/HSD_JObj* globals: un_804D7040, un_804D7044, un_804D7048, and un_804D704C.
Updated model iteration in un_80321178 from dereferencing a DynamicModelDesc triple pointer to using un_804D7040->models[i].

Added local static JObj setter clones for rotation Y, scale X/Y/Z, and translate X/Y/Z with assert line numbers and ftCo_800C6AFC invalidation behavior, apparently to reproduce generated code for this unit.
Split shared crowd-gasp logic into static un_80321C70_inline and kept un_80321C70 as a non-inlined function using #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop.
Factored repeated audio-stop-and-play logic in un_80321D30 into static un_80321CE8_caller while retaining separate un_80321CE8 behavior for a matching path.
Reworked control flow and temporary variables in un_80321EBC, un_8032201C, and un_8032233C, including explicit PAD_STACK usage and a goto end block in un_8032233C, to reach matching output.
Fixed vi1201v1 local scale setter helpers so HSD_JObjSetScaleY_2 writes jobj->scale.y and HSD_JObjSetScaleZ_2 writes jobj->scale.z instead of both writing scale.x.
smart_moves: The PR did not only rename callbacks; it also wired the renamed vi1202_OnEnter and vi1202_OnFrame through gm_803DA920 for MN_CUTSCENE_GIGADEFEATED, keeping symbols, headers, and scene data synchronized.
Using SceneDesc fields such as models, lights, and cameras clarified the archive layout enough to express vi1202_OnEnter naturally and improved .data/.sdata matching according to the bot report.
The local JObj setter clones preserve assert strings/line numbers and matrix invalidation behavior while allowing the compiler to emit the needed code shape for this scene-specific match.
The explicit non-inline pragma around un_80321C70 and the separate inline-like helper avoided duplicating logic manually while still matching both the standalone symbol and call-site codegen needs.
Changing player/object IDs and xC to u32 removed repeated casts in comparisons and ftLib calls, making the decompiled intent cleaner and closer to the generated code.
The small vi1201v1 correction fixed a likely copy/paste axis bug in helper code, even though the bot classified that function as still unmatched.
decomp_lessons: When a scene OnEnter match depends on archive data, first identify the archive symbol types: replacing opaque triple pointers with SceneDesc and HSD_Joint can unlock natural model/light/camera code.
Renaming scene callbacks should be propagated through config/GALE01/symbols.txt, the module header, and gmscdata MinorSceneHandler entries in the same PR to keep search and scene dispatch consistent.
For baselib JObj setters, local wrapper clones with exact assert file/line strings and ftCo_800C6AFC invalidation can be necessary when direct library calls or macros do not reproduce target codegen.
Unsigned typing for entity/player IDs can reduce cast noise and affect matching; fields compared against ftLib_80087460-style IDs should be checked for u32 suitability.
If one function must exist as a callable symbol but the same logic is needed inlined elsewhere, a static helper plus #pragma dont_inline on the exported wrapper is a viable matching tactic.
Small semantic helper bugs in adjacent vi files can show up during matching work; verify axis-specific setters assign x/y/z fields correctly when copying local HSD_JObjSetScale* clones.
naming_conventions: Scene callback names use the module prefix and role suffix: vi1202_RunFrame, vi1202_OnEnter, vi1202_OnFrame.
Unidentified functions remain under un_803xxxxx or fn_803xxxxx names until their role is clearer.
Local matching helpers use original SDK-style names with a _2 suffix, such as HSD_JObjSetScaleX_2 and HSD_JObjSetTranslateZ_2, to distinguish them from normal baselib APIs.
Static archive globals in this file retain address-derived names such as un_804D7040 while their C types are improved.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(24) in vi1202_OnEnter and PAD_STACK(8) in un_8032233C to reproduce stack layout.
Added u32 arg0_copy in un_80321EBC before ftLib_8008741C, indicating a temporary-copy/codegen-sensitive match adjustment.
Introduced goto end in un_8032233C so common tail assignments to data->x4, data->x0, and data->x8 are emitted in the expected shared tail shape.
Collapsed nested crowd-config range checks into a single OR condition in both the inline helper and non-inlined un_80321C70.
Used a ternary expression for max knockback selection in un_8032233C: kb_mag > data->x8 ? kb_mag : data->x8.
Kept exact __assert calls in local JObj setters with jobj.h line numbers 660, 661, 776, 791, 806, 932, 947, and 962.
Applied #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop around un_80321C70 to control inlining.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code increased to 49.32%, +0.08%, +3068 bytes.
Reported 6 new matches in main/melee/vi/vi1202: vi1202_OnEnter (+1504 bytes), un_8032233C (+174), un_80321EBC (+33), un_80321C70 (+6), un_80321D30 (+3), and un_8032201C (+3).
Reported improvements for vi1202 .data (+141 bytes, 0.00% to 98.23%), vi1202 .sdata (+14 bytes, 0.00% to 89.66%), and vi1201v1 un_8031FD18_OnEnter with unchanged percentage.
Reported a small vi1202 .sdata2 regression of -4 bytes, 75.00% to 66.67%.
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other vi scene OnEnter functions that still use opaque archive globals and could be converted to SceneDesc-based access.
Search for other local HSD_JObjSetScaleY_2 or HSD_JObjSetScaleZ_2 clones that accidentally assign scale.x.
Look for functions with duplicated crowd gasp range checks that could use the un_80321C70_inline matching pattern.
Audit remaining vi1202 symbols near un_80321900 through un_80322598 for additional naming opportunities.
Check whether the vi1202 .sdata2 regression from the bot report was later recovered.
